topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The roar of the crowd was a physical thing, vibrating up through my chest. I practically bounced on the balls of my feet, a wide, unrestrained grin splitting my face as I walked into the locker room. This was it. The team I’d dreamed about, the place where all my grueling early mornings would finally pay off. I slapped my teammate on the back, my laughter ringing out, feeling a surge of pure, unadulterated joy. When the coach finally called my name, a dizzying rush washed over me. He paused, a flicker of something unreadable in his eyes, before announcing the final roster. My smile faltered, replaced by a slow, sinking feeling as I realized my name wasn't on the list. The energy seemed to drain from the room instantly.

[story 2]

"We did it!" I shouted, throwing my arms wide as the final whistle blew, my whole body practically humming with victory. I couldn't stop moving, a giddy lightness propelling me across the field. I bumped shoulders with everyone, my voice high with excitement as I celebrated the win. Then, the official announcement came. My heart, which had been pounding a triumphant rhythm, suddenly stuttered. I looked at the team sheet, the bright colors suddenly seeming dull. A strange, hollow sensation settled in my stomach, heavy and cold, despite the warm afternoon sun. I managed a weak nod, trying to force a bright expression onto my features, but inside, everything felt strangely muted.

[story 3]

I practically skipped down the hallway, my steps light and quick, a bubbling sense of elation bubbling up from my core. Every glance I received was met with a dazzling smile, a refusal to let anything dampen the fantastic feeling currently radiating off me. I was practically vibrating with anticipation; the next level, the big league, was just around the corner. But when I finally sat down to review the team assignments, the air seemed to thin out. My hands, which had been gesturing wildly moments before, suddenly felt clumsy and heavy. A profound stillness took over, a strange emptiness where the high energy had been. I just stared at the paper, my breath catching in my throat.
